findings.summary: Fix 7's {data,error} wrapper is too invasive — requires updating 3 type declarations + 7 callsites. Replaced with try/catch returning safe fallbacks (zero caller changes).
Window.api types declared in 3 places (preload/types.ts, app.tsx, dialog-manage-agents.tsx local type). Any IPC return type change must update all 3.
dialog-manage-agents load and renderer consumeInitialDeepLinks both fire promises without .catch() — unhandled rejections if IPC fails.
submitAdd in dialog-select-mcp calls SDK via mutation but never persists to electron-store. Servers lost on restart.
electron-store is synchronous — per-namespace promise queue in ipc.ts serializes read-modify-write cycles from concurrent renderer calls.
Fix 7 error model: re-throw after logging to preserve caller try/catch blocks (dialog-manage-agents.tsx saveCustomAgents reverts optimistic state + shows toast on rejection)
Fix 8 serializedWrite must re-throw errors (not console.error + resolve undefined) so callers' existing try/catch blocks work
Fix 10 must extract validation functions to ipc-validation.ts and import in tests, not copy-paste like existing ipc-plugin-config.test.ts
All 3 getMcpServers callers and 1 getCustomAgents caller use (window as any).api — type changes won't catch at compile time. Fix 7 re-throw approach avoids this risk entirely.
7 additional fire-and-forget promise sites found across codebase — deferred to out-of-scope finding
cli.ts still calls window.api.installCli() with try/catch — handler already removed. Removing preload bridge changes error type but is safe (caught by existing try/catch)
Fix 6 pre-check not needed — rg confirms zero callers of preload bridge method
